title: OCR Error Detection Hotfix
body:
Summary of changes:
- Adjust input handling for the OCR error-detection LLM to rely on tokenizer-based length control rather than manual character-based splitting.
- Join multiple extracted text lines into a single newline-delimited string and pass that as input to the model, instead of passing an array of lines.
- Keep the output handling (corrected_lines) intact so downstream processing continues to map corrected lines back to line blocks and create spans with proper formatting.

Rationale:
- Tokenizers segment text by tokens rather than characters, so splitting logic based on #chars is brittle and unnecessary. By passing a newline-joined string, we let the tokenizer govern length constraints and improve robustness.
- Joining lines with newline characters ensures the model preserves line breaks when producing corrected output, simplifying alignment with the original lines.

Impact:
- Minimal code surface changes focused on the LLM text rewriting path. Downstream consumers (mapping corrected_lines back to text lines) remain unchanged.
- No changes to the structure of the returned data; the model still returns corrected_lines which are applied line-by-line.

Files touched (planned):
- marker/processors/llm/llm_text.py (LLMTextProcessor.process_rewriting)

Notes:
- If tests exist for LLM input format, they may need to be updated to reflect the input being a single newline-joined string instead of an array of lines.

step_plan.summary: OCR Error Detection Hotfix: adjust the LLM input path to use a newline-joined string for extracted lines, enabling tokenizer-based length control while preserving downstream output handling.

steps.operation: Refactor input construction in LLMTextProcessor.process_rewriting to join extracted_lines with newline characters into a single string and pass it as the extracted_lines payload to the model
steps.reason: Switch from per-line character-based splitting to token-based length control by supplying a single newline-delimited string to the LLM. This aligns with tokenizer behavior and preserves downstream mapping of corrected_lines to original lines.
steps.side_effects: Tests that assert input payload shape may need updates to reflect a string instead of an array; prompt construction changes may affect mocks or fixtures; downstream processing (mapping corrected_lines to spans) remains unchanged and should continue to pass.
